topic: Bayesian reasoning for interpreting product experiment results
learning_goal: Understand why a statistically significant result can still be a bad reason to ship a feature.
learner_context: I understand basic percentages and product metrics, but I get lost when people talk about priors, base rates, and false positives.
preferred_style: Use intuitive examples first, then introduce the precise terms. Avoid heavy notation unless it is necessary.
time_available: 25 minutes today, with one follow-up practice session tomorrow.
desired_depth: Practical enough to use in product decision meetings, not a full statistics course.
